name: Alexandra's Queen Bed Room Long-term Rental Only
description:
Thanks for visiting my Queen Bed Room for LONG-TERM rental! (31 nights minimum stay, at $1,250 per month) Extra guest is $5/night.

This bright room is on the main floor. Bathroom is nearby, not adjoining, and is shared. Guest will have a key; so no curfew. No other guests are permitted without pre-approval. Parking is available on street, with my parking permit. The location is SUPERB for all Downtown and Midtown activities.
neighborhood_overview: I love my Uptown neighborhood, which is within 2 to 3 miles of just about everything there is to do in Central Denver. There are four grocery stores, from the basic to the natural to the gourmet, in easy reach. There are a number of liquor and fine wine stores, multiple coffee shops and Internet cafes, Denver's many beautiful parks, and numbers of great and some renowned restaurants and eateries for breakfast, brunch, lunch, tea, dinner, and dessert. Churches, many beautiful and historical, abound, and many offer some of the most beautiful music and the most interesting services and dialogues around. Shopping opportunities, too, are many and varied, from the adjacent Downtown area, to nearby Cherry Creek Shopping Center and Cherry Creek North, to various outlet malls and stores further afield. Good antique and flea market venues are found throughout the Denver Metro area. Entertainment venues are close by. They include sports venues for the Denver Broncos, Nuggets, Outlaws, and Barbari

name: LoHi Secret Garden at Mulberry at Denver Cottages
description:
Enjoy our oasis in the city and stay at one of the founding Airbnb rentals.
We love to enjoy the famous Colorado weather and believe in indoor & outdoor living. We are located next to downtown and in the revitalized neighborhood of lower highlands.
Short walks to coffee shops, restaurants & microbrews, dispensary, Bug Theater & downtown.
We are 420 (outdoors only), LBGTQ friendly, allergy-free, fragrance-free & pet-free. UVC w/ Ozone sterilization.
neighborhood_overview:
For those who are interested in our local brews - we are within walking distance to three new breweries as well as a short drive to several other breweries.

**We are in an up-and-coming neighborhood & construction going on everywhere around us as well as all-around in Denver. Please be advised that there can be building sounds sometimes.


there is some history behind the cottages -
they were built in the 1890s and used to belong to a horse and carriage business that shuttled workers down to Union Station. our neighborhood has always been a working-class neighborhood starting with the Italians and then has welcomed many other waves of immigrants.

The Lower Highlands district is a thriving area due to the of proximity and the number of attractions that you can walk or bike to.

Bike paths are plentiful and you could tour the whole city on them.
you can rent a bike with a swipe of your credit card- Denver has a bike renta

neighborhood_overview:
Developed in recent years on a former Air Force base, the Lowry neighborhood is a mixed-use community that retains many historic buildings. Sidewalks and pedestrian paths connect several parks. Spruce House, as I call the place, is located about a one-quarter-mile walk (or drive) from the town center, which includes restaurants, coffee houses, a grocery store, shops and other commercial outlets, as well as a bus stop. description: This small home is a block from the heart of Denver's Santa Fe Arts District. Less than a Mile from downtown and the Convention Center.
neighborhood_overview:
One of the coolest and most conveniently located neighborhoods in Denver, this house is right off 6th and I-25, yet far enough from traffic to be quiet and homey.
The Santa Fe Arts District isn't as polished and gentrified as many other neighborhoods in Denver. No big chains and fast-food restaurants on Santa Fe or South Broadway, but plenty of breweries, restaurants, theatres, and more!
One caveat, the neighbors on both sides are musicians. I've never had any noise complaints from tenants, but want to warn those who may be light sleepers or sensitive to noise, this Art District home may not be right for you (especially on the first friday of the month).

name: Urban Nest | hot tub | dog friendly | bikes & walk
description: Newly remodeled & charming 1 bedroom beautifully bright basement apartment w/ exposed brick, private entrance in a fantastic location. Includes  an oasis-like courtyard, hot tub, grill, & bikes. Walking distance to parks, Botanic Gardens, coffee shops, & restaurants! Up to 2 dogs are welcome! Ideal for 2 people, possible for 3. No smoking inside/outside, including the courtyard. E-Vaping & edibles are encouraged! Private/separate basement unit & owner lives upstairs. Only the back door is shared
neighborhood_overview: This is one of the oldest neighborhoods of Denver. All the houses have character and charm. It is fun just to go for a walk. We live just a block from many great restaurants, and our go- to coffee shop "St Marks". There are restaurants all along 17th street! Just 5 blocks from both - City Park and Cheeseman Park, where we love to take the dogs, go for a run, rent a bike or paddle boat for an hour or so, or picnic on a summer sunday night to listen to live music. The Zoo and Botanic Gardens are in walking distance. During the summer months the Botanic Gardens hosts amazing, intimate concerts in their outdoor amphitheater. If you're here during this time you should check out their calendar. There are several other concert venues in close proximity, too. Less then a 10 minute commute to both Downtown Denver and Cherry Creek North or Cherry Creek Mall. Close to several other old town neighborhoods with small shops and tons of restaurants.

neighborhood_overview:
Virginia Village is a friendly residential neighborhood in a central location with easy access to DU to the west, Cherry Creek directly north, and the Denver Tech Center due south. Neighboring areas have top rated restaurants within a 10 minute drive - Sushi Den & Bird in Platte Park - Jax Fish House in Glendale - Max's Gill & Grill & Perdida in Wash Park - Cherry Creek Grill & Cherry Cricket in Cherry Creek.

name: Friendly 91 license 24390
description: This is more like a townhome, with no one above or below you. My place is within walking distance to many dispensaries, Sloan's Lake, restaurants, Joy Ride Brewery, bars, and dining. My place is 23 minutes or 14 miles from Red Rocks Amphitheater, 20 minutes or 13 miles from Dick's Sporting Goods Park, 11 minutes or 7 miles from Coors Field, and 9 minutes or 3 miles from downtown Denver.
neighborhood_overview:
Make yourself at home and relax, you are among friends and neighbors!
When you exit the complex through the front gates, take a right to get to Sloans Lake, 25th St. Joyride Brewery, many dispensaries and restaurants.
You will notice the Mexican bakery, hair stylist and nail salon across the street (Sheridan) on your left before the 7-11 store. Other eateries are down along the lake on your right. Rupert's is my favorite, and is only open for breakfast and lunch.
Barbershop and one day spa are also down Sheridan just past Starbucks, as is Northern Lights dispensary. The other way on 29th is my favorite day spa A new spirit 4840 w. 29th.
Catch the #28 Eastbound bus in the covered bus shed by the lake across the street from 7-11. Fare is $2.60 and includes a transfer. This takes you downtown where you can catch the free 16th St. Mall Shuttle. Catch the #28 bus Westbound to return home on 15th St. The drivers are all very helpful as are other riders.

description:
Welcome to my 2Br/1Ba in Santa Fe Art District with the best reviews/score for the price in Denver!
Whether you are on business, a couple(s), or a family, the space, amenities, and location are perfect for a long stay! A $9-13 Uber to almost anywhere including downtown, the Convention Center, Mile High Stadium, and entertainment. Uber, scooter, or walk to restaurants, breweries, art galleries, and dispensaries. neighborhood_overview: Santa Fe Art District is an eclectic mix of art galleries, their owners, restaurants, bars, coffee shops, and families. The alleyway behind my home is one of the densest areas of street art/graffiti/murals in Denver.  One block over from my street is Santa Fe Ave, lined with shops, art galleries, restaurants, and bars. It's an up-and-coming neighborhood  (still transitioning) with mostly families and artists and some young professionals. Going to the east, the neighborhoods are more transitional, but changing fast as they are now anchored by the newer and huge Anshutz Medical Campus. neighborhood_overview: Capitol Hill is Denver's most densely populated neighborhood. It was once the home of Denver's elite and origination of Foursquare architecture in Denver. Today it consists of historic mansions, apartments and condo buildings. Because of the concentration of multi-family uses and the urban character of the neighborhood, parking is at a premium. The area is strongly influenced by the Colorado State Capitol and Downtown Denver which is adjacent to the northwest boundary of the neighborhood. Commercial uses are typically located on major arterials such as Colfax Avenue, East 8th Avenue, East 14th Avenue, East 13th Avenue, Lincoln Street and Broadway. A considerable number of mansion properties have been converted to office uses over the past several years due to zoning exemptions offered to historic structures which are zoned for residential uses.

neighborhood_overview: Capitol Hill is a quintessential Denver neighborhood, a place where politicos and hipsters cross paths. It is Denver's most densely populated neighborhood. It was once the home of Denver's elite and origination of Foursquare architecture in Denver. Today it consists of historic mansions, apartments and condo buildings. Because of the concentration of multi-family uses and the urban character of the neighborhood, parking is at a premium. The area is strongly influenced by the Colorado State Capitol and Downtown Denver which is adjacent to the northwest boundary of the neighborhood. Commercial uses are typically located on major arterials such as Colfax Avenue, East 8th Avenue, East 14th Avenue, East 13th Avenue, Lincoln Street and Broadway. A considerable number of mansion properties have been converted to office uses over the past several years due to zoning exemptions offered to historic structures which are zoned for residential uses.

name: Modern Carriage House Loft in Popular Platt Park
description:
No smoking on property and No 420. Our modern carriage house is centered in a vibrant historic Denver neighborhood. The interior is bright and airy with vaulted ceilings and huge windows. The house features a kitchen, living area and separate bedroom.

There are many award winning nearby restaurants and breweries (even a couple distilleries) all in walking distance. The light rail station is a convenient 10 minute walk, the bus station is around the corner. Uber is available in minutes.
neighborhood_overview:
The carriage house is one block off "Antique Row", a section of Broadway with many small antique shops and a several musical instrument shops.
Pearl Street in the center of the neighborhood has a number of local restaurants, small shops and art galleries. On Sunday mornings in the summer, two blocks of Pearl Street are closed off for the weekly farmer's market. Ideal for walking, there are many people out with their dogs. You are likely to encounter: Huskies, Bernese Mountain dogs, German Shepherds, Labs and other large breeds.
Our neighborhood was once part of the city of "South Denver". There are many homes from the late 1800s. In the 1920s, many bungalows were added. In the center of Platt Park neighborhood, is the Denver city park, Platt Park. The old mayor of South Denver's house sits at the north end of the park.
